message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to know the man's reaction after the reporter asks several questions in a row.
Looking at the video around 01:50-02:18:
The reporter asks: "What about your biological parents? Did they influence your works?"
The man looks uncomfortable.
Reporter: "I mean, I don't sense solitude in your works. Are you trying to seek redemption through your paintings?"
The man looks annoyed, says "We'll talk again. I'm all yours." and walks away abruptly.
This matches option C: Gets angry and ends the interview.

Transcript: "What about your biological parents? Did they influence your works?" ... "I don't sense solitude in your works. Are you trying to seek redemption through your paintings?" ... "We'll talk again. I'm all yours." (sarcastic/abrupt ending).
Visuals: Man's facial expression changes to annoyance/anger, he stands up and leaves.

Evidence 1: Transcript of reporter's questions about biological parents and redemption.
Evidence 2: Visual of man looking annoyed/angry.
Evidence 3: Visual of man walking away and ending the interview.

D. Sad - He looks more annoyed/angry than sad.
